title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Melrose attorney Henry Rappa Jr. charged with embezzling client funds, practicing law while suspended.
Rappa allegedly misappropriated $660,000 from a client's trust account, violating court orders to stop practicing.
Rappa faces charges in Malden District Court including embezzlement, larceny, and unauthorized practice of law.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning car after a crash at Bedford Toll Plaza, New Hampshire.
State trooper and security detail members pulled Mirman from the wreckage; Governor Ayotte also assisted.
Mirman thanked rescuers and hospital staff on social media, sharing humor despite his serious injuries.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, charged after attacking a NH State Trooper in Dover parking lot with stick and tool.
Trooper was in parked cruiser when Newcomb allegedly became verbally aggressive, punched him, and attempted to take his gun.
Newcomb faces multiple charges including felony criminal threatening, DWI, and disorderly conduct; both men treated for minor injuries.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett runs the Boston Marathon supporting Mass General in memory of his second mother, Sharla Sieve.
Mallett honors Sieve, who died from metastatic colorectal cancer, by completing the marathon for a second year.
Mallett aims to support a Mass General program through a fundraiser initiated by Sieve's daughter, Olivia.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1, per The Athletic.
Brown's potential trade is linked to salary-cap implications and his connection with Patriots coach Mike Vrabel.
The Eagles have sought a first-round pick for Brown, while the Patriots show interest in upgrading their roster.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' starring Amanda Seyfried, is streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is now available to stream on Netflix.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' is available on PBS, featuring narrations from George Clooney.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox win home opener against Padres 5-2, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin breaks a slump with an RBI single, helping Boston's victory.
Johan Oviedo placed on 15-day injured list due to elbow strain, awaiting MRI results.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offseason program starts April 20 with conditioning drills and meetings as phase one.
Organized Team Activities (OTAs) for the Patriots are scheduled for May 27, 29, June 1-2, 4, 8-9, and 11.
A mandatory minicamp will conclude the offseason program from June 15-17 before training camp in July.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots projected to select edge rushers in both first and second rounds of NFL draft, per ESPN mock draft.
Texas A&M's Cashius Howell and Illinois' Gabe Jacas are the edge rushers ESPN predicts Patriots will draft.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes drafting best available players, not solely based on team needs.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox win 5-2 against Padres in Fenway Park home opener, marking a fresh start after struggling early season.
Sonny Gray pitches six strong innings; Willson Contreras' home run and Caleb Durbin's RBI spark Red Sox offense.
Red Sox improve to a 2-5 record; players and manager Cora express optimism despite ongoing concerns with team performance.
